front: Artist: A woman gazes into a mirror shattered by a bullet hole; title in Futura Oblique with misaligned letters
Installation: At the Hirshhorn Museum covering the floor and escalator; 2012 exhibition in D.C.
Artist: Supreme clothing brand copied this artist's visual style (white Futura text on red background)
Artist: Called Supreme "a ridiculous clusterfuck of totally uncool jokers"
Artist: We Won't Play Nature to Your Culture — appropriated magazine image of a woman with leaves over her eyes
Artist: When I Hear the Word Culture I Take Out My Checkbook — alternate title from Hans Johst quote; divides picture of Howdy Doody into three panels
Artist: Twelve (2004) — video installation with actors playing art critics and political strategists yelling slogans like "Get out of my face!"
Artist: Remote Control: Power, Cultures, and the World of Appearances — book about TV/film's influence; cover shows hand holding empty Reese's wrapper
Artist: Illustrated Stephen King's My Pretty Pony with tiny red stopwatches and a stainless-steel cover with digital clock
Artist: Curated exhibition Picturing Greatness with photos shot solely by men (Rodchenko, Robert Frank)
Artist: Sculpture Justice — faux-Neoclassical statue of J. Edgar Hoover kissing Roy Cohn (who is in high heels)
Artist: Uses Futura Bold Oblique typeface (and sometimes Helvetica Ultra Condensed); both are sans-serif fonts
Artist: American feminist artist; white text on red backgrounds over black-and-white photographs
